focus: CONSOLIDATION: separate one-time DB repair from durable prevention, define reindex/retention/daemon fixes, and cover memory_embedding_reconcile risks.

graphEvents.label: One-time reconcile repair is separate from durable prevention fixes
Reindex completion should mark target-profile vector-present rows success
Retry retention must not fail never-attempted clean pending rows
Retry retention config must be dynamic inside long-lived daemon
Reconcile apply requires transaction, active-shard, dimension, and idempotency guards
Q1-Q6 one-line answers are ready for final synthesis

label: Separate one-time reconcile repair from durable prevention
Reindex completion should mark only target-profile vector-present rows success
Retention must exclude never-attempted clean pending rows; post-drain ordering alone is insufficient
Daemon retry config must be dynamic; bridge-only reconnect does not reload module-scope constants
memory_embedding_reconcile must fail closed on concurrent drain, partial vectors, profile mismatch, and zero-dim vectors
Rows are marked success only when the active target shard and vec_<dim> coverage both exist for the same memory_index id
Retry retention must not convert retry_count=0 clean pending rows to failed
Reconcile apply is idempotent: a second dry-run after apply reports zero vector-present stale rows
Compiled reindex completion flips active embedder and job status without status repair
Large clean pending backlogs can still be parked after a small post-drain batch unless retention excludes retry_count=0 rows

direction: Bridge-only env reload as the sole durable fix
reason: Retry retention values are module-scope constants today, so bridge reconnect cannot repair stale constants unless retry config is also read dynamically inside the daemon.
